diagrams.title: Linear SVM Maximum Margin Classifier
diagrams.description: Shows a 2D representation of a linear Support Vector Machine that separates two classes of data points (circles and squares) using a decision hyperplane. The diagram illustrates the maximum margin concept where the hyperplane is positioned to maximize the distance between the closest points of each class.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the core concept of SVM as a maximum margin classifier and identifies support vectors as the critical data points that define the decision boundary

diagrams.title: Kernel Trick Visualization
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how SVM handles non-linearly separable data using kernel functions. Shows data points in original 2D space that cannot be separated by a linear boundary, then illustrates transformation to a higher-dimensional space where a linear hyperplane can separate the classes.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the kernel trick and how SVM can create complex decision boundaries by implicitly mapping data to higher dimensions without computing the transformation explicitly

diagrams.title: SVM Classification Process
diagrams.description: Shows a new data point being classified by the SVM model. The diagram displays the decision region on one side of the hyperplane labeled as one class, and the opposite region as another class, with an example test point being categorized based on its position relative to the hyperplane.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates how SVM makes predictions for new data points by determining which side of the decision boundary the point falls on
